summary: Selected and implemented a bounded checker diagnostic improvement for the native self-hosted compiler path: record literal missing-field failures now become structured diagnostics instead of falling through as generic checker text.

self_hosted: src/Compiler/Checker.clasp emits `Record literal for ... is missing field ...` checker messages.
src/Compiler/Driver/Frontend.clasp exposes checker summaries through `checkSourceText` and project check exports.
runtime/claspc.rs classifies selected self-hosted checker text into machine-readable native diagnostics.
bootstrap: test/Main.hs still asserts bootstrap-style diagnostic codes such as `E_RECORD_MISSING_FIELDS`.
clasp-compiler.cabal still references `deprecated/bootstrap/src`, but that source tree is absent in this checkout, so implementation work targeted the live native/self-hosted path.
tests: scripts/test-native-claspc-diagnostics.sh is the focused scenario-level gate for native parser/checker diagnostics.
test/Main.hs contains richer Haskell diagnostic assertions, but the referenced bootstrap library sources are not present in this workspace.

bounded_improvement.behavior: When checking `User { name = "Ada" }` against `record User = { name : Str, active : Bool }`, `claspc --json check` now emits `E_RECORD_MISSING_FIELDS` with phase `checker`, context `main`, target `User`, expected `active`, actual `missing field`, and a primary span at the record literal use.
bounded_improvement.expected_human_output: Pretty diagnostics include `CLASP_DIAGNOSTIC phase=checker code=E_RECORD_MISSING_FIELDS ... context="main" target="User" expected="active" actual="missing field"` before the checker message.
bounded_improvement.expected_json_output: JSON diagnostics include code, phase, message, context, target, expected, actual, file, line, column, and primarySpan for the missing record field.

verification_commands: timeout 240 env -u CLASP_CLASPC -u CLASPC_BIN CLASP_PROJECT_ROOT="$PWD" bash scripts/test-native-claspc-diagnostics.sh
commands_not_run: bash scripts/verify-all.sh was intentionally not run per task instruction.
rustfmt --check runtime/claspc.rs and cargo test were attempted but unavailable because rustfmt/cargo are not installed directly on PATH.
follow_ups: If the bootstrap Haskell source tree is restored in this checkout, mirror the native record-missing-field assertions in `test/Main.hs` for parity.
Consider the same structured treatment for `Record literal ... includes unknown field ...` and `Record update ... includes unknown field ...` as a separate small diagnostic slice.
residual_risks: The diagnostic classifier still derives structure from checker message text; a future typed diagnostic value from `src/Compiler/Checker.clasp` would be more robust.
The absent `deprecated/bootstrap/src` tree prevents direct bootstrap diagnostic source inspection in this task workspace.
